zh.Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingd: 英國英格蘭素可安莫林
zh.Angmering, Sussex, , England: 英國英格蘭素可安莫林
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabell Elizabeth Bilton於1433年出生在英國素可安莫林時，她的父親Sir Edward Bilton26歲，母親Margaret Cook22歲。她於1454年與Sir John Palmer在英國英格蘭素可安莫林結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1495年在英國英格蘭素可安莫林享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabell Elizabeth Bilton于1433年出生在英国素可安莫林时，她的父亲Sir Edward Bilton26岁，母亲Margaret Cook22岁。她于1454年与Sir John Palmer在英国英格兰素可安莫林结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1495年在英国英格兰素可安莫林享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素可。

nameMeanings.name: Bilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places in Northumberland and Yorkshire called Bilton, from an Old English personal name Billa + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. There is also a Bilton in Warwickshire, of which the first element is probably Old English beolone ‘henbane’ (compare Belton ).

variants.name: Belton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Belton, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, and Suffolk. The first element, bel, is of uncertain origin; the second is Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
English: occasionally a variant of   Bilton  , a habitational name from Bilton in Warwickshire.
Irish: from the Norman name Weldon , relatively common in Ireland, which has sometimes been Gaelicized as de Bhéalatún and re-Anglicized as Veldon and Belton.

nameMeanings.name: Isabell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabell Elizabeth Bilton was born in 1433, in Angmering, Sussex, England, her father, Sir Edward Bilton, was 26 and her mother, Margaret Cook, was 22. She married Sir John Palmer in 1454, in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1495, in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabell Elizabeth Bilton est née en 1433, à Angmering, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward Bilton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret Cook, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Palmer en 1454, à Angmering, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1495, à Angmering, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabell Elizabeth Bilton 1433 in Angmering, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Edward Bilton 26 und ihre Mutter Margaret Cook 22. Sie hat 1454 in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Sir John Palmer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1495 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Sussex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell Elizabeth Bilton è nata il nel 1433 nel Angmering, Sussex, England, sua padre, Sir Edward Bilton, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Margaret Cook, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Palmer nel 1454, nel Angmering, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1495, nel Angmering, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabell Elizabeth Biltonが1433年にイギリススセックスアングメリングで生まれた時，父Sir Edward Biltonは26歳，母Margaret Cookは22歳であった。1454年にイギリスイギリススセックスアングメリングでSir John Palmerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1495年にイギリスイギリススセックスアングメリングで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススセックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabell Elizabeth Bilton(이)가 1433년 영국 수세스 앤그머잉에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Edward Bilton(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Cook(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1454년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 앤그머잉에서 Sir John Palmer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 앤그머잉에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell Elizabeth Bilton nasceu em 1433, em Angmering, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward Bilton, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Cook, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Palmer em 1454, em Angmering, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1495, em Angmering, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabell Elizabeth Bilton родилась в 1433 г. в местности ангмиринг, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Edward Bilton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Cook, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Palmer в 1454 г. в местности ангмиринг, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1495 г. в местности ангмиринг, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabell Elizabeth Bilton nació en 1433, en Angmering, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward Bilton, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret Cook, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Sir John Palmer en 1454, en Angmering, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1495, en Angmering, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
